I am deeply, deeply sorry for the loss of your father. I wish more than anything that I could be there with you right now, to hold your hand, to sit with you in the silence and to make sure you eat something even when you don't feel like it.

Your father was such a good man, a man of dignity and wisdom, and I know how much he meant to you. Losing a father is losing a piece of your foundation, and I will not pretend I understand fully what you are walking through, but please know my heart is walking with you every step of the way.

My love, please be strong, not in the way that does not let you cry, but in the way that lets you lean on God and the people who love you. Anything you need, anything at all, do not hesitate to ask.

How are you holding up, truly? Please write back when you are ready.

I love you so much.

My dear Grandfather,

It is with the deepest joy and gratitude that I stand here today, to celebrate you turning ninety years old. Ninety. What a beautiful, full life, and we are blessed beyond measure to share it with you.

I can clearly remember running through your garden as a child, you teaching me how to whistle through a blade of grass, and the long walks where you would tell me stories of your boyhood; lessons quietly tucked inside every tale. You taught me patience, you taught me to listen, and you taught me that the smallest kindness is the loudest thing a person can do.

I respect you for your unwavering integrity, your gentle humour, your love for our family. Even now, in your golden years, you continue to bring such joy to everyone around you, your laughter still fills every room you walk into.

Happy ninetieth, Grandfather. May we have many more.

Friends and neighbours,

We are gathered today to say goodbye to a man who fed this little corner of town for more years than most of us can count. Ted, our local greengrocer, our friend, our familiar face every Saturday morning behind the apples and the carrots and the ridiculous prices on his ridiculous chalkboard signs.

Ted knew everyone by name. Mrs. Patel and her grandkids, the runners coming back from their loops, even the dog from number 42- he had a name for that dog, I'm not even kidding. He was warmhearted in a way that is becoming a bit rare these days. I'll never forget the time he quietly packed two crates of veg every Friday for the homeless shelter, and refused to let anyone mention it.

He is survived by his beloved Margaret, ninety-two years young. Our love and strength to her.

Cheers to you, mate. You'll be missed.

My dear love,
I know this last week has been so hard for you since you lost your job. I see how heavy your shoulders look and I just want you to know - it's going to be okay. Truly. You have been through tougher things than this and you came out stronger every time.

For sure you'll find a new job sooner or later. And you know what, maybe it'll be even better than the old one - maybe this is life pushing you toward something bigger, something you actually deserve. You are talented, you are hardworking, any company would be lucky to have you. Don't doubt yourself, please.

In the meantime, please don't worry about money. I'll support you all the time, we are a team and we have always been a team. We've got savings, we've got each other, and that's enough for now. Just rest, breathe and take your time. I love you so much.

9f5025499cf64a52.text:
Dear Friend

You know how much our friendship means to me, all these years and all the memories we have built together. Please find a place in your heart to forgive me. Love you loads.

A regretful friend

ebdf645fca5f70ff.text:
Dear John,

I’m standing here in our little backyard, with the fairy lights up and our people watching, and my heart is so full I can barely speak. Five years ago, in that rainy coffee shop, you sat across from me with your soaked jacket and that crooked smile, and somehow I knew. I just knew.

You are the kindest part of my every day. You make me laugh when you dance in the kitchen with the wooden spoon, you sing off-key on purpose because you know it cracks me up. Even on the hard days, you find a way to make the room feel warm.

“Stay close,” you always whisper at night, and I always do. I promise to hold your hand through every storm and every sunny day, through the loud and the quiet. I choose you, John. Today, tomorrow, and all the days after.

Love always,
Your wife

70c242ac5ea2d8ce.text:
Dear friend, I hope this letter finds you in some kind of peace.

I write this letter to send my deepest condolences to you and your family on the loss of your mom. I cannot imagine the pain you are felling right now, the kind of pain that has no real words for it. A mother is a mother, no one can ever replace her, and the empty space she leaves is one only love can slowly soften.

All I know is that all will be fine in due time, even though right now it feels like the world has gone too quiet. Take it one day at a time, please don't push yourself to be strong every minute. Cry when you need to cry, rest when you need to rest. Lean on those who love you.

Know that you are in my thoughts and prayers in this difficult time. I am always one phone call away. Whatever you need.

You raised me with patience and love and prayers, and i let you down in front of your own face. Please forgive me, i promise to do better in the future.

32714b4a32372215.text:
Dear my friend,

I'm still smiling about the surprise you left at my door. A whole set of Eckhart Tolle's The Power of Now books - just sitting there, waiting for me to open the door and find them. I had no idea, and I cannot tell you how much that gesture brightened my day. It was such a thoughtful, quiet thing to do, just like you.

The funny thing is, the books arrived at exactly the right moment. I have been overwhelmed lately, more than I have shown, and reading them has helped me breathe again. Slowly, I'm finding calm. With every page I feel a little lighter.

Thank you for noticing me without me having to say anything - that has always been our quiet kind of connection, hasn't it. I'm so grateful for you, my friend. Let's catch up soon - maybe coffee and a long walk, like before.

With warmth and love,
Your friend

27b2ddfaf7074070.text:
Friends, family,

We are gathered today to remember our dear friend, Matthew. I knew Matthew for many years and I can honestly say I never met a man more determined to look on the bright side of life. He was, in every sense, a born optimist- and that quiet sunshine of his was an encouragement to all of us, especially on the days the rest of us could not find any.

Matthew was a loving husband and a wonderful father to Emily and Rebecca; the three of them were the centre of his world. He was also a true stalwart of our local Church, raising more money for it than most would have thought possible, with his charitable work and his endless fund-raising ideas.

Amongst the Sunday School children he was much loved- for his humour, his compassion, and his listening ear. We will all miss him terribly.

Rest easy, mate.
X.

925c132fa33711b3.text:

Today, as i stand in front of you and our families, i want to take this opportunity to thank you for being the most wonderful husband, father and friend a woman could ever ask for. Sixteen years, my love. Sixteen years of growing together, building together, and loving each other through every season life has thrown at us.

I love and appreciate you for everything you have done for me and our little family. You have been our rock, our laughter, our shelter when the rain came. You loved me when i was difficult to love, and you held our children with such tenderness that my heart still melts watching you with them.

I want us to grow old together, Pedro, sitting on a porch somewhere with grey hair and tired knees, still teasing each other like the first day. I will never let go. We are forever, my love. Always and always.

My beloved Mom

Words cannot describe how immeasurably hard it is to be standing here today, paying tribute to you. I can clearly remember the very first time I understood what kind of woman you were; I was a small girl, watching you do everything alone, working two jobs and still showing up to school plays with that warm laughter of yours. As a single mother, you gave me your absolute best, and even on the hardest days, you made me feel safe and loved.

Now many years later, and I can truly say, you shaped me. You shaped me through your zest for life, your fearless way of starting again, your habit of finding joy in the smallest of things. The young woman I am today is built on every sacrifice you ever made.

As I take this next chapter alone, may the love we shared give me strength and unwavering fortitude, for the future.

My friend, my apologies, I am truly sorry. Wow, it is hard to write this letter, I am full of shame. I realized that I spoke without thinking, that I let my mood walk into the room and speak before my better self could. My words ended up hurting you, that was not my intention, but I can see now how careless I was. 

You have always taught me that strength doesn't always roar, sometimes it is gentle, steady and full of grace. And I let myself roar in a way that I should not have. 

Remember that we are friends, real friends, and real friends own up. No more pretending, no more brushing it off. Cherish the truth of our friendship as I do. I love you and I wish you a peaceful heart toward me. 

Much will change from this point forward in how I speak. Do not be afraid, do not be discouraged, our friendship is bigger than my mistake.

edcac8569a78ee04.text: To say sorry to you it's a big and a heavy thing for me but I have to do it because you deserve it I want to acknowledge that I made a mistake and I take full responsibility for what I did, no excuses no blaming no nothing. I'm sorry, I'm truly sorry from deep down inside me. What happened was, I was tired and stressed and I let those feelings come out on you instead of dealing with them like a grown person should, that is not a justification just an explanation of what was going on with me. 679af7101c8f168f.text:
Dear Ray,
Today I'm writing to say thank you, from the bottom of my heart. Times flies so fast - it's already been a week since that crazy night at the bar, and I still cannot believe how things could have gone. I still remember Gary going off with that group of strangers and then disappearing into the night. We were panicking - so afraid and uncertain. But you, Ray, you tracked him down, made sure he was safe, and brought him home in one piece. You did not have to do that, but you did. It could have ended so badly, and i don't want to imagine the alternative. Gary is also thankful, he really is, but he's so embarrassed he can barely look you in the eye right now. He'll come around. Ray, you're the best friend a couple could ask for. I promise to take care of you the same way if you ever need me. I love you, brother.

79d57467499a004b.text:
Dear Lena,
Happy 25th, my friend. I can’t believe we’re already here.
We met in 6th grade art class. Both of us got paint on the ceiling trying to flick our brushes around like we were in some music video. The teacher was so mad. You laughed so hard you slid off your stool, and that was kind of it for me, I knew we were going to be friends.
You drag me to karaoke even when I’m terrified to sing. You pick songs you know I secretly love, and you sing the high notes so I don’t have to. You always do that, find a way to make me feel brave without making me feel pushed.
You have the biggest heart of anyone I know. You make every person in a room feel special, like they’re the one you came to see. I’ve watched you do it for years, and it never stops amazing me.
Happy birthday, Lena. The world is a little louder and a lot brighter because you’re in it.
Love you,
Me

bd702d9cc78cf327.text: Today I write this letter not for a happy reason, but because I was deeply saddened to hear that your father passed away. Your father was such a kind and positive person, and the news has not stopped sitting in my chest since I heard it. I still remember when we was just kids and I would come over to your house, and at that moment we had no idea how much his small advices would shape us. I still remember every detail of how he would call us into the kitchen, sit us down, and tell us to always work hard and respect people. His smile and his kindness touched everyone around him, not just family but neighbors, strangers, anyone who walked through your gate. My favorite memory was the day he fixed my bike chain in the driveway and refused to let me leave without lemonade. He is always in my thoughts and prayers, and I will pray for him daily. I am here for you, my friend, always. And I believe his love will last forever.

f2ccb25c61b552b5.text:

Please let me know when you are free. The lunch is on me.

ccf2651fa5bc9c6b.text:
Dear friend,

I'm really sorry for what you are going through right now. Losing your mom is one of the heaviest things life can ask anyone to carry, and my heart has been with you since I heard the news.

I keep thinking about her gentle voice, the way it could calm a whole room without her even raising it. She had this way of making everyone who walked through her door feel like they belonged there, like they had always been part of the family. I will never forget how she used to insist on feeding me before I left, even when I said I was not hungry.

Please remember that her warmth and kindness will keep living in the hearts she touched, including yours and mine. We will carry her forward together.

I'll be here whenever you need me - to talk, to cry, or just to sit quietly. We can go for a long walk like we used to.

With warmth and love,
Me

cebe3e7e0fbbee15.text:
Hi Jennifer,
I would just like to take a moment to say I am so sorry about my behaviour in that Italian restaurant last night. I am, of course, referring to the way I shouted at you across the table when all you were doing was trying to be a good friend.
It meant a lot that you were even willing to listen about my job, but in my head I twisted it and decided you were dismissing my worries and being insincere- which was completely unfair on you. I have been under a lot of stress and pressure at work lately, and I just lost it. I know this is no excuse. I have spoken to myself about it and I know I was a bit off my head, mate.
I really value our friendship- it is much appreciated and important to me. Please forgive me! I will do anything to get us back on an even keel. Your friendship means the world to me.
From,
X.

989a5a1bef743aee.text:
My lovely son, Thando. Today, I am writing this letter to you because there are some things a parent must say plainly, and I would rather say them now than let silence sit between us. I am so sorry, my boy, that I could not buy you the PlayStation 5 I had promised you for your birthday. I love you so much, and you must know that I would have moved mountains if I could have. You have been so respectful and patient from the very onset until today, and that is very rare to happen these days, especially when kids now know their rights.

Things did not go according to how I had planned them, my son. Bills came when I did not expect, and the money I had set aside for your gift had to cover them. I pray that you understand, and I pray that God grants me the strength and the means to make it possible for your next birthday. Never confide in disappointment, but always know your father is doing his best.

Take good care of yourself, my love. Always know that you will always be my baby, no matter how old you might be.

3602d80ba41453b3.text: Hey friend I know today is your birthday and I just want to take this moment to say a few words about you. Do you remember when we tried to bake that cake for your mum and ended up burning the whole kitchen and pretending nothing happened until she walked in and just stared at us. That story tells everyone exactly who you are, brave, a little chaotic and somehow always fun to be around. Better days are coming and I'll be cheering you through every single one. Now everyone please raise your glass with me to my favourite person, cheers!

9c9f7bee860b04e6.text: My best friend. I am truly sorry for what happened, my friend. We have been friends since we were friends, and I cannot stand the thought of being on bad terms with you. I did not mean for things to escalate that way and I have replayed it in my head over and over. Having you in my life has been amazing and the idea of damaging this friendship has been weighing on me. I am writing this because I am ashamed of how I behaved and I want you to know that I see it. Please find it in your heart to forgive me, even if it takes time. I know how much our friendship means to both of us and I hope we can get through this together. I wish you would speak to me again, even just one short message. I pray that we will laugh again the way we used to. Always know that I love you and I will always have your back no matter what.

9a0999201a6c6e07.text:
I want to offer my deepest condolences to you and the family, I cannot imagine the pain that has settled in your home this week. Losing him has left a quiet that feels louder than any noise, and I know that pain personally now. All I can do is sit with you in it and be present.

I have so many memories of him I do not even know where to start. The way he would tell the same joke at every braai and laugh at it harder than anyone else. The way he insisted on driving us home that one rainy night because he did not trust the taxi. The little things that I never thought would become memories until suddenly they were the only thing left.

He was a good man and you are a good family, and I am here for you in any small or big way. Friends share the bad times the same way we share the good. I love you.
